title: CDIAC Meeting Summary - April 11, 2024
summary: The Community Depository Institutions Advisory Council met on April 11, 2024, and reported that the economy remains resilient despite persistent inflation driven by strong consumer demand, with a notable bifurcation between upper- and lower-income groups. Council members expressed doubts about the near-term feasibility of achieving the 2% inflation target, noting that services inflation persists due to labor costs while goods inflation moderates.
key_points: Economy remains surprisingly resilient despite inflationary pressures and persistent challenges
Lower-income households experiencing greater difficulty keeping up with rising prices compared to upper-income groups
Inflation has moderated from peak but remains stubbornly high, driven by both labor costs and strong consumer demand
Goods inflation is slowing while services inflation persists due to associated labor costs
Housing prices have moderated but remain restrictive for most consumers; multifamily permit issuance varies by district
Labor costs are moderating from 6-7% pay increases last year to 4-5% this year
Council members expressed doubts about near-term feasibility of 2% inflation target
Insurance costs rising across most business forms with diminished availability of insurance products
Technology costs increasing 10% year-over-year in 2024, up from prior 2-3% averages
why_it_matters: The why_it_matters is accurate but could be strengthened to better justify archival value. Consider noting specific unique intelligence from this meeting that distinguishes it from routine economic commentary.
macro_context: The meeting summary reflects regional economic conditions as of April 2024, providing qualitative context for official data. The bifurcation between upper- and lower-income households aligns with Fed concerns about distributional impacts of inflation. The shift from goods to services inflation dominance is a key macro theme that influences the Fed's assessment of inflation stickiness and policy duration.
important_numbers: 100+ immigrants hired by one Japanese manufacturing plant in one District

notes: This is a historical CDIAC meeting summary from April 2024, approximately 2 years prior to the current date. CDIAC is an advisory council that provides input to Fed leadership on regional economic conditions affecting depository institutions. While the content is relevant to macro catalysts (inflation dynamics, labor markets, housing), its age significantly limits direct applicability to current market conditions. The document represents qualitative intelligence from regional banking perspectives rather than official Fed policy signals. Treat as historical context rather than actionable market intelligence.
